title: Glean leans on enterprise graph grounding for agent planning
claim: Glean's agentic engine emphasizes adaptive planning grounded in an enterprise graph, learned relationships, and organizational context so agents can take on more complex work.
source_snippet: Agents are grounded in the Enterprise Graph and adapt plans as new insights emerge.
implication: ProductOS should use workspace memory and canonical-source retrieval to ground agentic research instead of letting agents improvise from stale context.
followup_questions: Which ProductOS memory surfaces should be mandatory context before a research agent can recommend scope or positioning?
